name: American Continental Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank established in 2003, serving Asian communities in Los Angeles and Seattle/Bellevue with checking, savings, loans, and business banking services.
description_long:
American Continental Bank was established on October 6, 2003, by a group of local businessmen with the specific mission of providing specialized financial services to the Chinese community. The bank is headquartered in the greater Los Angeles area with additional locations in Seattle/Bellevue, positioning itself as a community-focused institution rather than a national chain. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to applicable limits.

American Continental Bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking products. On the deposit side, they provide personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs). Their lending products include commercial real estate loans, apartment loans, construction loans, equipment loans, commercial business loans, SBA small business loans, and residential mortgage loans. Additional services include online banking with bill pay, wire transfers, cashier checks, direct deposit, night depository, ACH processing, debit/ATM cards, safe deposit boxes, and 24-hour telephone banking via (855) 389-8998.

What distinguishes American Continental Bank is its deliberate positioning as a community bank serving specific Asian demographic segments in Southern California and the Pacific Northwest. The bank emphasizes relationships and long-term community engagement rather than rapid growth or technology-first banking. Their management team and board consist of experienced local businessmen with deep community ties, and the bank explicitly prioritizes employee development and training. The institution maintains a stated commitment to "honesty" as a foundational principle and promotes personal, face-to-face banking relationships.

However, potential customers should note several limitations. The bank has limited geographic presence (only greater Los Angeles and Seattle/Bellevue areas), minimal digital/mobile banking presence on their website, and no mention of competitive interest rates, promotional offers (customers must visit branches to learn about promotions), or modern fintech features. The website content is sparse and dated (copyright 2019), and there is no information about account minimums, fee structures, or loan terms. As a small community bank, they likely cannot compete on rates or technology with larger national banks or online banking platforms.
services: Online banking with bill pay and internal transfers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Established community bank with 20+ years of operational history (founded October 6, 2003)
Bilingual and culturally-specific banking services for Asian communities in two major metro areas
Comprehensive lending products including SBA small business loans, commercial real estate, and residential mortgages
Online banking with bill pay, internal transfers, and balance information access
Strong management team with deep local business and community relationships
cons: Very limited geographic footprint (only Los Angeles and Seattle/Bellevue areas); not available nationwide
Minimal digital presence and outdated website (last copyright update 2019); no mention of mobile app or modern fintech features
No published information about account minimums, interest rates, or fee structures on website
No promotional details visible online; customers must visit branches to learn current offers
Small institution size may result in less competitive rates and fewer advanced banking services compared to major national banks

contact.address: 17700 Castleton St, City Of Industry, CA 91748

best_for: Business owners and entrepreneurs in the Chinese/Asian community in Los Angeles or Seattle wanting culturally-aligned banking
Customers seeking relationship-based community banking with in-person service and local decision-making
Small business owners needing SBA loans, commercial real estate financing, or equipment loans from a community lender
Residents in greater Los Angeles or Seattle/Bellevue wanting FDIC-insured deposits and basic to intermediate banking services
diagnosis: Best for small business owners, entrepreneurs, and residents within the Los Angeles and Seattle/Bellevue areas who value community banking relationships and culturally-aligned service, particularly those serving or part of the Asian business community. Primary caveat: geographic availability is severely limited, and the bank's minimal digital presence and lack of published rate/fee information means prospective customers must visit a branch in person to evaluate product competitiveness.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 17700 Castleton St, City Of Industry, CA 91748
